sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.🛠 准备与协作.lin.message: Before you write the first requirement: setting up the AI environment, the coding environment, and how several AI agents split the work. This part covers the prep that gets me into working shape.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.💡 灵感与可行性.li.message: Where ideas come from, how to validate them, how to judge feasibility, how to do the research. This part covers how to work with me—before you build—to sharpen a vague notion into something worth doing.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.📋 需求分析.link.message: Turning an idea into clear, unambiguous, verifiable requirements. The five entries here are all about "I made the call for you," but the failure modes differ: a gap is a question mark you can see, and I guess instead of asking; ambiguity is a fork you might see, and I pick one reading without flagging it; an implicit assumption is a premise you don't even notice, and I quietly fill in a default; a placeholder is missing real input, and I fake one to paper over it; a spec is a complex task that deserved a written one, and I just start coding by feel.
sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.📋 需求分析.link.description: The generated-index page description for category '📋 需求分析' in sidebar 'wikiSidebar'

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.🏗 概要设计.link.message: How to break the system apart, split it into modules, and settle technology choices. This part covers how to lean on my strength at comparing trade-offs at the architecture level—rather than letting me slap a single design on you.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.⌨️ 编码实现.lin.message: The phase where code actually gets written—and where pitfalls cluster most densely. This part covers session management, the context window, CLAUDE.md, rolling back and correcting—the things that decide whether I'm a help or a burden.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.✅ 测试.link.g.message: Code that I write and that "looks right" is not the same as code that is right. This part covers how to use tests to give me a verification loop I can run myself—one that emits a clear pass/fail signal.

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.📓 案例库.link..message: Earlier sections cover "how I might crash"; this one collects "people who actually crashed this way" — public, verifiable incidents and postmortems, each mapped to the pitfall(s) it bears out. One bar for inclusion: it really happened, it has a reliable public source, and it maps to a pitfall in this book; hearsay and unverifiable rumor don't make it in. Read it as a crime-scene photo for the mechanism reasoning: first see "why this happens" in the phase entry, then come here for "what it looked like when it actually happened, and what it cost."
sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.📓 案例库.link..description: The generated-index page description for category '📓 案例库' in sidebar 'wikiSidebar'

sidebar.wikiSidebar.category.🧰 模板与清单.lin.message: The entries tell you what not to do; this section hands you things to use directly — permission and security configs, review/security/governance checklists, and task and workflow templates. Each compresses the book's best practices into a copy-paste artifact you can adapt.
